title: LF04 Skillset
subtitle: Carry out a protection-needs analysis in your own work area
intro: LF04 makes information security concrete and actionable in everyday work.
The focus is on protection needs, risks, measures, and robust documentation for operations.

subsections.title: Classify the basics of information security
items: Explain the protection goals of confidentiality, integrity, and availability in the work context
Identify information assets in the work area
Distinguish security domains in the company appropriately
Use security-relevant terms correctly in the team
Explain the importance of information security for business operations

subsections.title: Clarify responsibility, roles, and ownership
items: Distinguish the tasks of IT, specialist departments, and management in security matters
Classify reporting paths for security incidents
Clearly delineate your own role in security processes
Coordinate security tasks in a team-oriented way
Record responsibilities cleanly in tickets and notes

subsections.title: Differentiate laws, standards, and baseline protection
items: Separate legal requirements from internal standards
Describe the basic idea of BSI baseline protection in operations
Classify norms and policies for security work
Apply security policies in daily work
Recognize compliance-relevant aspects early

subsections.title: Assess threats, hazards, and damage scenarios
items: Name current threats to your own area
Derive hazards from concrete assets
Structure damage scenarios by impact
Estimate likelihood and damage roughly
Prepare risk factors so they can be used for prioritization

subsections.title: Respond to social engineering and identity theft
items: Recognize typical social engineering patterns
Check phishing indicators in daily work
Handle suspicious contacts correctly
Classify identity misuse as a risk for business processes
Communicate preventive behavior rules in the team

subsections.title: Derive technical and organizational measures
items: Propose suitable technical safeguards
Formulate organizational security contributions
Prioritize measures by effectiveness and effort
Define minimum protection for typical workplace scenarios
Integrate measures into existing business processes

subsections.title: Apply the security process and policy
items: Describe phases of a security process in a practical way
Transition from policy to protection-needs assessment
Plan process steps for the work area in a traceable way
Document results in line with the process
Mark deviations and residual items cleanly

subsections.title: Carry out protection-needs assessment methodically
items: Assign objects and protection-need categories correctly
Assess protection needs for processes and information
Explain the reasoning for classifications professionally
Derive criticality in a traceable way
Document protection-need decisions consistently

subsections.title: Protection needs for workstation software and clients
items: Determine protection needs for workplace software in use
Identify client-side protection requirements
Classify configuration and operational risks on clients
Assess dependencies between software, client, and data flow
Justify minimum measures for regular operations

subsections.title: Protection needs for data exchange and mobile storage media
items: Assess risks in data exchange via mobile storage media
Apply transport, encryption, and access rules
Follow approval processes for mobile storage media
Report security incidents involving media in a structured way
Assess secure alternatives to mobile storage media

subsections.title: Calculate availability, damage, and priority
items: Translate availability values into downtime
Prepare damage estimates for decisions
Derive priorities from risk and impact values
Explain calculation results to management and specialist departments
Combine quantitative results with qualitative assessment

subsections.title: Security documentation and communication
items: Document protection-need decisions briefly and traceably
Communicate residual risks and measures transparently
Formulate security messages to fit the audience
Prepare results for audits and follow-up questions
Secure follow-up of measures in the team
